title: Listed on awesome-europe
author: GeiserX
body:
Hi! 👋

Your project has been listed on [awesome-europe](https://github.com/GeiserX/awesome-europe), a curated list of open source software relevant to the European Union and EEA.

If you'd prefer not to be included, just let me know here and I'll remove it — no questions asked.

Thanks for building great European open source software!

title: Laravel 11.x Compatibility
author: joniand
body: I see there's already a pull request for that. Any idea when it might get merged?

title: Changing the downloaded file name ?
author: dgillier
body:
Hello,

The downloaded file is name user.json, anyway to dynamically change (like real_user_name.json) ?

Thanks, 
Denis

title: show only some relationship attributes
author: dgillier
body:
Hello,

Thanks for this great package !

I tried to hide some of the relationship attributes by using your $gdprVisible property with just the fields I want  visible, working ok for the User model but not for the relationships.
(I  tried to add some of the relationship  attributes like 'bookings.name'  in $gdprVisible, but it's not working)

Anyway to do that ?
Thanks, Denis

body: Are there any plans to update this to Laravel 9?

Is there a way to decrypt data?

Thank you

Hi,

title: Authentication Mistake
author: cedvict
body:
Hi Guys

I have some error when I try to get user by email

Have settings below

use EncryptsAttributes

protected $encrypted = [ 'email'];

I made query User::where(['email' => 'some@domain.com'])->first();

It doesn't wwork

title: Laravel 8 incompatibility issue
author: maicol07
body:
Laravel 8 introduced the possibility to have a Models folder inside the app directory.

Currently, the package imports App\\User but if the User.php file is in the Models directory the correct namespace is App\\Models\\User. This raises an exception.

https://github.com/sander3/laravel-gdpr/blob/b5560965348eb2a7cdba83947ac2ce8ceb9a00c7/src/Console/Commands/Cleanup.php#L5

Is it possible to login using an encrypted email? Because the login doesn't run over the User model and gets the email attribute. Is there a way to achieve this?

title: How to hide relation attributes?
author: robertogallea
body:
Hi, I am not able to hide specific attributes from relations. I have tried the following:

1. in `User` set `$gdprHidden = ['relation.attribute']` 

2. Add `Portable` trait to `Relation` and inside it set `$gdprHidden = ['attribute']` 

What am I missing? Thank you.

title: Decryption not working with toArray()
author: ameerElsherif
body:
Hi there,

Automatic decryption of values only works when accessing model attributes like this:
`$model->attribute`

However, returning a model instance or using `$model->toArray()` returns encrypted values.

Any suggestions?
